char.name: Simeon
char.bio: Second son of Leah. Quick to draw the sword when my sister's honor is on the line.

friends.name: Leah
friends.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Judah
friends.bio: Fourth son of Leah, and this time, I will praise the LORD. Learning my way, for better and for worse. 🦁

friends.name: Reuben
friends.bio: Firstborn of Jacob, eager, but unsteady as water. Found mandrakes in the field for my mother.

friends.name: Dinah
friends.bio: Daughter of Leah and Jacob. I went out to see the women of the land, and everything changed. 💔

friends.name: Hagar
friends.bio: Maidservant from Egypt. Life took a hard turn, but God saw me by the spring in the wilderness. He is the God who sees. 🌅

friends.name: Ishmael
friends.bio: Son of Abraham. God promised to make me a great nation too. Living free in the open desert with a bow in hand. 🏹

friends.name: Abram
friends.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Sarai
friends.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

friends.name: Isaac
friends.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

friends.name: Rebekah
friends.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

friends.name: Esau
friends.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

posts.author: CHAR_177
posts.body: A man took our sister, used her like a common harlot, and then had the gall to send his father over to buy her off us afterward, like she was a heifer to be haggled over. And my father sat there and said nothing. My father is always quiet now. So Levi and I answered for this family. We told them, sweet as you like, that we could never give our sister to uncircumcised men, but that if every male in their city would be circumcised, then we would intermarry and become one people together. They agreed. Of course they agreed. They wanted our flocks and our daughters and our wealth. Every man in that city went under the knife that same day, trusting us completely. We were not building an alliance. We were digging a grave, and they climbed down into it smiling.
posts.highlight: Simeon and Levi answer deceitfully, trapping the men of Shechem into circumcision (Gen 34:7-24)

author_info.name: Simeon
author_info.bio: Second son of Leah. Quick to draw the sword when my sister's honor is on the line.
